key_insight: A hybrid GNN-LSTM surrogate can learn the nonlinear static response of additively manufactured short-fiber thermoplastics directly from microstructure topology and history-dependent cell behavior.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, physics-informed prediction of stiffness and stress-strain response for complex SFT parts, supporting digital twins and screening of weak microstructural regions.
method: Reconstructed microstructures from micro-CT and partitioned them into Voronoi-based cells.
Ran nonlinear FE simulations per cell with matrix damage to generate local stress-strain responses.
Trained a GNN-LSTM to encode microstructural connectivity and mechanical evolution over loading history.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matches unseen FE results with about R² ≈ 0.98 and reduces computation by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of the reconstructed microstructures, so extrapolation to new materials or processing conditions should be validated carefully.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
